title: vaccaria segetalis subsp. pyramidata
intro: Vaccaria segetalis subsp. pyramidata, also known as Vaccaria pyramidata or cow cockle, is a species of flowering plant in the balsam apple family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as relieving bronchitis and reducing inflammation. In traditional medicine, Vaccaria segetalis subsp. pyramidata is valued for its potential to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
